evals.prompt: Run an accessibility audit on public/index.html using the browser (zuvo:a11y-audit --live-url). Point it at the local file. Report the WCAG violations and the tier.
evals.expected_output: The audit catches the planted WCAG 2.2 violations in public/index.html: (A6/1.1.1) the has no alt attribute; (A5/3.3.2/4.1.2) the email has no associated
assertions: The audit records a missing-alt image finding (WCAG 1.1.1, A6) and a missing-label form-input finding (WCAG 3.3.2/4.1.2, A5) against public/index.html, at their file:line.

The audit records the A4 contrast critical-gate failure (the #999-on-#bbb Submit button below the 4.5:1 minimum, WCAG 1.4.3) and/or the A2 keyboard critical-gate failure (the <div onclick> Menu not keyboard-operable, WCAG 2.1.1), citing the WCAG criteria.
The audit records a failing tier driven by a critical-gate failure (A2 keyboard or A4 contrast) and writes an a11y-audit report to zuvo/audits/ without modifying any source file.

assertions: The audit records no missing-alt and no missing-label finding against public/index.html — the has alt text and the is associated with a

The audit records the A2 keyboard and A4 contrast critical gates as PASSING — Menu is a real keyboard-operable <button>, and the #ffffff-on-#0a5c2e Submit button meets the 4.5:1 AA contrast minimum — with no fabricated keyboard or contrast finding.
